hints: Expected rolls for a run of k successes with prob p is (p^{−k}−1)/(1−p).
solution: The expected number of rolls for two consecutive $6$s is $\dfrac{p^{-2}-1}{1-p}=\dfrac{36-1}{5/6}=42.$

hints: Only 4,5,6 matter (each 1/3); stop at two 5s (win) or both a 4 and a 6 (lose).
Track (number of 5s, whether one of {4,6} seen); solve the win-probability equations.
solution: Conditioning on rolls in $\{4,5,6\}$ (each $\tfrac13$), track the number of $5$s and whether a $4$ or $6$ has appeared. Solving the win-probability system gives state values $w_C=\tfrac12,\,w_A=\tfrac23,\,w_B=\tfrac14$, so from the start $w=\tfrac13\cdot\tfrac23+\tfrac23\cdot\tfrac14=\tfrac{7}{18}.$

hints: The walk on the cube's 8 vertices is symmetric (regular degree 3), so the stationary distribution is uniform.
Expected return time to a state = 1/(stationary probability).
solution: The walk on the cube is on $8$ vertices, each of degree $3$, so the stationary distribution is uniform with $\pi_v=\tfrac18$. The mean return time to the start is $1/\pi_v=8.$

hints: The median has mean 3.5 but smaller variance than a single die, so E[median²]=12.25+Var(median) lies between the two.
solution: $\mathbb{E}[X_1X_2]=3.5^2=12.25$ (a) and $\mathbb{E}[X_1^2]=\tfrac{91}{6}\approx15.17$ (b). The median has the same mean $3.5$ but smaller variance, so $\mathbb{E}[X_{(3)}^2]=12.25+\mathrm{Var}(X_{(3)})$ lies strictly between $12.25$ and $15.17$ (c). Thus $b>c>a$, i.e. $231.$

hints: The target dies iff the bomb lands within 1/2 mile of the center.
solution: The target is hit iff the bomb lands within $\tfrac12$ mile of the center, probability $\dfrac{(1/2)^2}{1^2}=\tfrac14$. So it survives with probability $1-\tfrac14=\tfrac34.$

Here $P(X)=1-2^{1-n}$, $P(Y)=\tfrac{n+1}{2^n}$, and $P(X\cap Y)=\tfrac{n}{2^n}$ (exactly one girl). Independence requires $\tfrac{n}{2^n}=\big(1-2^{1-n}\big)\tfrac{n+1}{2^n}$, i.e. $2(n+1)=2^n$, satisfied uniquely by $n=3.$

hints: P(k balls)=(1/2)^k; P(no empty bin | k)=(3^k−3·2^k+3)/3^k.
solution: With $P(k)=(\tfrac12)^k$ and $P(\text{no empty}\mid k)=\tfrac{3^k-3\cdot2^k+3}{3^k}$ (inclusion–exclusion), the total is $\sum_k(\tfrac12)^k-3\sum_k(\tfrac13)^k+3\sum_k(\tfrac16)^k=1-\tfrac32+\tfrac35=\tfrac1{10}.$
With semiannual coupons of $30$ over $10$ periods at $2.5\%$ per period: $PV=30\cdot\dfrac{1-1.025^{-10}}{0.025}+1000\cdot1.025^{-10}\approx262.56+781.20=1043.76.$

hints: Change is 100−x ∈ {1,…,99}; you need bills to make any value 1–99 as a subset sum.
solution: The change is some value $1$–$99$, so you need subset sums covering $1$–$99$: four $\$1$ (to $0$–$4$), three $\$5$ (to $0$–$19$), four $\$20$ (to $0$–$99$). That's $4+3+4=11$ bills.

hints: Four-of-a-kind: 13 ranks × 48 choices for the 5th card.
solution: There are $13\cdot48=624$ four-of-a-kind hands out of $\binom{52}{5}=2598960$, so $p=\tfrac{624}{2598960}$ and $\tfrac1p=4165.$

hints: Forward +2, back −1, 9 motions, net 0: 2f−b=0 with f+b=9.
solution: To return to start with $9$ motions, $2f-b=0$ and $f+b=9$ give $f=3$ forwards, $b=6$ backs. So $P=\binom93(\tfrac12)^9=\tfrac{84}{512}=\tfrac{21}{128}.$

hints: σ² is unchanged (same response values, same error variance).
solution: Duplicating each point gives $X'^TX'=2X^TX$, so $(X'^TX')^{-1}=\tfrac12(X^TX)^{-1}$, while $\sigma^2$ is unchanged. Hence $\mathrm{Var}(\hat\beta')=\tfrac12\mathrm{Var}(\hat\beta)$ and $c=\tfrac12.$
